IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: WE PASSIONATELY CHAMPION & ADVOCATE FOR EVIDENCE-BASED POLICIES THAT PROTECT CHILDREN , IMPROVE HEALTH AND SAVE THE MOST LIVES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: WE PASSIONATELY CHAMPION & ADVOCATE FOR EVIDENCE-BASED POLICIES THAT PROTECT CHILDREN , IMPROVE HEALTH AND SAVE THE MOST LIVES.
IRS990.Desc: (DOMESTIC) ADVOCACY/ COMMUNICATIONS/ RESEARCH/ YOUTH SERVICES:SUPPORTED THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE'S CIVIL LAWSUIT AGAINST THE MAJOR TOBACCO COMPANIES; ADVOCATED FOR FUNDING FOR TOBACCO PREVENTION AND TOBACCO CESSATION PROGRAMS AS WELL AS EXPANDED INSURANCE COVERAGE FOR CESSATION SERVICES; CONTINUED TO SUPPORT IMPLEMENTATION OF THE FOOD AND DRUG ADMINISTRATION'S AUTHORITY OVER TOBACCO PRODUCTS; PLANNED AND IMPLEMENTED STATE-WIDE ADVOCACY CAMPAIGNS THAT HOLD ELECTED OFFICIALS ACCOUNTABLE FOR TOBACCO ISSUES, PROMOTE THE PROVEN SOLUTIONS THAT REDUCE TOBACCO USE AND GENERATE VOTER SUPPORT FOR INITIATIVES THAT ADVANCE TOBACCO-CONTROL.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: INTERNATIONAL PROGRAMS:MANAGED AN INTERNATIONAL PROGRAM TO SUPPORT THE ADOPTION OF STRATEGIES TO REDUCE THE CONSUMPTION OF TOBACCO GLOBALLY; STAFF MANAGE AN INTERNATIONAL GRANT PROGRAM THAT PROVIDES FUNDS TO ORGANIZATIONS IN LOW AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES TO ADOPT AND IMPLEMENT TOBACCO CONTROL LAWS, REGULATIONS, POLICIES AND PROGRAMS, INCLUDING TAX AND PRICE MEASURES, ADVERTISING BANS, CONSUMER WARNINGS AND SMOKE-FREE WORKPLACES AND PUBLIC PLACES LAWS; AND PROVIDED ASSISTANCE TO GRANTEE AND PARTNER ORGANIZATIONS IN STRATEGY DEVELOPMENT, CAMPAIGN IMPLEMENTATION, COMMUNICATIONS AND MEDIA OUTREACH, POLICY RESEARCH AND LEGAL DRAFTING AND ANALYSIS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: GLOBAL ADVOCACY INCUBATOR:AN INTERNATIONAL PROGRAM THAT MANAGES A UNIT THAT TRAINS AND ADVISES OTHER ORGANIZATIONS ON THE CONDUCT OF HEALTH POLICY CAMPAIGNS IN LOW AND MIDDLE INCOME COUNTRIES.

OrganizationBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: DP STRATEGIES LLC DBA BROWN STRATEGY GROUP

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TFKAF ENGAGES IN POLITICAL CAMPAIGN ACTIVITY ONLY TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY FEDERAL LAW AND THE APPLICABLE STATE OR LOCAL LAWS. IN 2022, TFKAF ENGAGED IN POLITICAL ACTIVITIES FOR CANDIDATES FOR ELECTED OFFICE

AccountActivitiesOutsideUSGrp.TypeOfActivitiesConductedTxt: GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOACTED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION
GRANTS TO RECIPIENTS LOCATED IN REGION

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE TOBACCO-FREE KIDS ACTION FUND (TFKAF) INTERNATIONAL GRANTS SUPPORT NON-LOBBYING AND LOBBYING ACTIVITIES OF NON-GOVERNMENTAL AND CIVIL SOCIETY ORGANIZATIONS WORKING TO HELP PASS, IMPLEMENT AND ENFORCE TOBACCO CONTROL POLICIES IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME FOREIGN COUNTRIES. TO EFFECTIVELY CONTROL AND MONITOR THE RECIPIENT'S USE OF THE GRANT FUNDS, THE ACTION FUND MAINTAINS THE FOLLOWING POLICIES & PROCEDURES: - THE ACTION FUND DOES A CAREFUL PROGRAMMATIC AND FINANCIAL REVIEW OF ALL GRANT APPLICANTS AND PROPOSALS TO ENSURE THAT PROJECT OBJECTIVES AND ACTIVITIES ARE IN LINE WITH TFKAF GOALS AND FIT WITHIN THE APPLICANT ORGANIZATION'S CAPACITY AND EXPERIENCE. - THE ACTION FUND DOES NOT FUND ANY GRANT ACTIVITIES THAT MIGHT INCLUDE SUPPORTING OR OPPOSING CANDIDATES FOR ELECTED OFFICE. - THE ACTION FUND REQUIRES DETAILED INFORMATION FROM EACH GRANT APPLICANT SO THAT IT CAN FOLLOW THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY ANTI-TERRORIST FUNDING GUIDELINES REGARDING CHARITABLE GRANTS TO FOREIGN ENTITIES OR INDIVIDUALS. AMONG OTHER THINGS, THE ACTION FUND ENSURES, PRIOR TO ISSUING ANY GRANT, THAT NEITHER THE RECIPIENT ORGANIZATION NOR ANY OF ITS DIRECTORS, TOP EXECUTIVE STAFF, MAJOR FUNDERS OR MAJOR RECIPIENTS OF ORGANIZATION GRANTS ARE LISTED ON ANY OF THE MAJOR DATABASES OF PERSONS AND ORGANIZATIONS LINKED WITH TERRORISM. - THE INTERNATIONAL GRANT AGREEMENTS, THEMSELVES, CONTAIN SPECIFIC PROVISIONS TO PROTECT AGAINST GRANT FUNDS BEING USED TO SUPPORT TERRORIST ORGANIZATIONS OR ACTIVITIES, TO SUPPORT OR OPPOSE ANY CANDIDATES FOR POLITICAL OFFICE, OR IN VIOLATION OF ANY APPLICABLE LAWS. - EXCEPT FOR SOME RELATIVELY SMALL GRANTS WITH SHORT TIME FRAMES, THE ACTION FUND BREAKS UP ITS PAYMENT OF THE TOTAL GRANT AMOUNTS INTO INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS TO HELP ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE GRANT AGREEMENTS AND SO THAT THE ENTIRE GRANT AMOUNTS ARE NEVER AT RISK OF IMPROPER USE OR DIVERSION. THE ACTION FUND ALSO REQUIRES INTERIM REPORTS, INCLUDING FINANCIAL REPORTING, THAT MUST BE RECEIVED AND APPROVED TO ENSURE PROPER USE OF RECEIVED GRANT FUNDS BEFORE ADDITIONAL INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS ARE MADE. AS PART OF THE FINANCIAL REPORTING, GRANTEE ORGANIZATIONS ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT ANNUAL FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND/OR AUDIT DOCUMENTS. - FOR LARGER GRANTS, THE ACTION FUND UNDERTAKES SITE VISITS, AS WELL, TO ENSURE THE PROPER USE OF GRANT FUNDS, TO CHECK THE INTERNAL FINANCIAL CONTROL SYSTEMS OF THE RECIPIENT ORGANIZATIONS, AND TO ENGAGE IN OTHER OVERSIGHT AND SUPPORT ACTIVITIES. - AT THE CONCLUSION OF ALL GRANTS, GRANTEE ORGANIZATIONS ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT FINAL NARRATIVE AND FINANCIAL REPORTS, WHICH ARE ANALYZED TO ENSURE THAT ALL GRANT ACTIVITIES HAVE BEEN COMPLETED, ALL FUNDS HAVE BEEN EXPENDED AND EXPENDED IN ACCORDANCE WITH U.S. GOVERNMENT AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT REGULATIONS AND RESTRICTIONS, AND ALL OTHER REQUIREMENTS HAVE BEEN FULFILLED UNDER THE TERMS OF THE GRANT.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF PENNSYLANIA INC

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE TOBACCO-FREE KIDS ACTION FUND (TFKAF) PROVIDES GRANTS TO 501(C)(3) AND 501(C)(4) NONPROFITS IN THE UNITED STATES THROUGH BOTH GENERAL-PURPOSE AND PROJECT-SPECIFIC GRANTS, AND ALSO SOMETIMES MAKES CONTRIBUTIONS TO STATE OR LOCAL BALLOT MEASURE COMMITTEES. EXCEPT FOR THE CONTRIBUTIONS TO BALLOT MEASURE COMMITTEES, GRANTS ARE GIVEN ONLY TO ORGANIZATIONS THAT TFKAF HAS WORKED WITH IN THE PAST AND WILL CONTINUE TO BE WORKING WITH ON AN ONGOING BASIS. THIS FAMILIARITY WITH GRANT RECIPIENTS, AND THE CONTINUING WORKING RELATIONSHIPS, MAKES GRANT OVERSIGHT MUCH EASIER AND MORE DIRECT. TO EFFECTIVELY CONTROL AND MONITOR THE RECIPIENT'S USE OF THE GRANT FUNDS, THE ACTION FUND ALSO MAINTAINS THE FOLLOWING POLICIES & PROCEDURES. TFKAF GENERALLY MAKES GRANTS TO SECTION 501(C)(3) AND 501(C)(4) NONPROFITS THAT ITS REGIONAL ADVOCACY DIRECTORS HAVE WORKED WITH IN THE PAST AND ANTICIPATE WORKING WITH IN THE FUTURE. MOST OF THE GRANTS DISTRIBUTED TO U.S. SECTION 501(C)(4) ENTITIES HAVE A CLAW-BACK PROCEDURE. EACH FORMAL GRANT PROPOSAL IS SUBMITTED ON FORMS PROVIDED BY TFKAF THAT REQUIRE DETAILED INFORMATION TO ALLOW FOR A CAREFUL EVALUATION OF THE GRANT PROPOSAL AND THE RECIPIENTS ABILITY TO USE THE GRANT FUNDS EFFECTIVELY, AS WELL AS FORMAL CONFIRMATION OF THE APPLICANT'S 501(C)(3) OR 501(C)(4) STATUS. GRANT REQUESTS ARE EVALUATED BY THE REGIONAL ADVOCACY DIRECTORS, THE VICE-PRESIDENT FOR STATE ISSUES AND OTHER RELEVANT TFKAF STAFF, SOMETIMES IN CONSULTATION WITH OTHERS FAMILIAR WITH THE APPLICANT OR THE PROPOSAL'S SUBJECT. AFTER CLARIFYING ANY ISSUES RAISED RELATING TO A PROPOSAL, INCLUDING POSSIBLE REVISIONS TO THE PROPOSAL, THOSE REVIEWING THE PROPOSALS RECOMMEND WHETHER OR NOT THE GRANT SHOULD BE MADE. THE PROPOSALS AND RELATED GRANT DOCUMENTS FOR RECOMMENDED GRANTS ARE REVIEWED BY THE TFKAF VICE PRESIDENT FOR STATE ISSUES, GENERAL COUNSEL, AND VICE PRESIDENT FOR FINANCE & ADMINISTRATION. UPON THEIR APPROVAL, THE GRANT AGREEMENT DOCUMENT IS SUBMITTED TO TFKAF'S PRESIDENT FOR FINAL APPROVAL AND SIGNATURE. AFTER THE CHECK IS DISBURSED, THE RELEVANT ACTION FUND REGIONAL ADVOCACY DIRECTOR WORKS WITH THE GRANTEE ON AN ONGOING BASIS TO CONFIRM THAT THE GRANT FUNDS ARE USED CONSISTENTLY WITH THE GRANT AGREEMENT. THE GENERAL PURPOSE GRANT LETTERS PROVIDE THAT THE FUNDS MAY BE USED ONLY FOR ACTIVITIES 501(C)(4) NONPROFITS MAY LEGALLY ENGAGE IN AND MUST BE USED EITHER FOR GENERAL PURPOSE OF PREVENTING AND REDUCING TOBACCO USE OR ITS HARMS OR FOR ONLY FOR THE SPECIFIED PROJECT.

THE PRESIDENT'S COMPENSATION IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD WHO WORK AT SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS AND ARE FAMILIAR WITH COMPARABLE COMPENSATION FOR SIMILARLY QUALIFIED INDIVIDUALS. THE BOARD EVALUATES THE PERFORMANCE OF THE PRESIDENT ANNUALLY AND MAKES RECOMMENDATIONS ON SALARY INCREASES BASED ON PERFORMANCE AND THE AVERAGE RATE OF INFLATION. COMPENSATION IS CONSIDERED AND APPROVED IN AN EXECUTIVE SESSION BY THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS. PERIODICALLY, HR REVIEWS COMPENSATION OF THE EXECUTIVE MANAGEMENT TEAM AND STAFF TO ENSURE THAT COMPENSATIONS ARE IN LINE WITH SIMILARLY QUALIFIED INDIVIDUALS IN COMPARABLE POSITIONS AT OTHER NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS.

PURSUANT TO A WRITTEN COST-SHARING AGREEMENT, THE TOBACCO-FREE KIDS ACTION FUND SHARES EMPLOYEES WITH THE CAMPAIGN FOR TOBACCO-FREE KIDS ("CFTFK," EIN: 52-1969967), AN IRC SECTION 501(C)(3) ORGANIZATION. UNDER THE AGREEMENT, THE TOBACCO-FREE KIDS ACTION FUND REIMBURSES CFTFK FOR ALL COSTS RELATED TO THE EMPLOYEE'S WORK ON ITS BEHALF, INCLUDING RELATED TAXES. CFTFK, AS THE COMMON PAYMASTER, SUBMITS ALL TAX FILINGS FOR THE SHARED EMPLOYEES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 8B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: NO COMMITTEES ARE AUTHORIZED TO ACT ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FINANCE & ADMINISTRATION STAFF, IN CONSULTATION WITH OUR OUTSIDE ACCOUNTING FIRM, PREPARES THE 990 FORM EACH YEAR. ONCE IT IS COMPLETED AND APPROVED BY THE VICE-PRESIDENT FOR FINANCE & ADMINISTRATION AND THE PRESIDENT, IT IS PRESENTED TO THE FINANCE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS (WHICH INCLUDES THE FINANCE COMMITTEE CHAIR) AND TO THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS ALONG WITH KEY UNDERLYING DATA AND RECORDS. THE FINANCE COMMITTEE AND BOARD ARE GIVEN READY ACCESS TO RELEVANT STAFF AS WELL AS THE ACCOUNTING FIRM IN THE EVENT THAT THE COMMITTEE HAS QUESTIONS OR WANTS TO REVIEW ANY DATA OR RECORDS THAT WERE NOT INITIALLY PROVIDED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TOBACCO-FREE KIDS ACTION FUND HAS A FORMAL, WRITTEN CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY FOR STAFF AND BOARD THAT IS PERIODICALLY REVIEWED AND, AS NEEDED, UPDATED AND REVISED. THE WRITTEN POLICY IS PERIODICALLY DISTRIBUTED TO ALL STAFF AND BOARD MEMBERS, AND ALWAYS DISTRIBUTED WHEN ANY SIGNIFICANT CHANGES ARE MADE. ALL NEW STAFF MEMBERS AND NEW BOARD MEMBERS ARE GIVEN A COPY OF THE WRITTEN CONFLICTS OF INTEREST POLICY, AND IT IS AVAILABLE ON THE CAMPAIGN'S INTERNAL COMPUTER NETWORK. THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY CLEARLY STATES THAT STAFF AND BOARD MUST DISCLOSE ANY OF THEIR EXISTING OR PLANNED ACTIVITIES THAT MIGHT CONSTITUTE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND THAT THE CAMPAIGN RESERVES THE RIGHT TO TAKE ANY REASONABLE ACTION TO RESOLVE A CONFLICT SITUATION, INCLUDING THE POSSIBLE TERMINATION OF AN EMPLOYEE AND/OR BOARD MEMBER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE PRESIDENT'S COMPENSATION IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD WHO WORK AT SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS AND ARE FAMILIAR WITH COMPARABLE COMPENSATION FOR SIMILARLY QUALIFIED INDIVIDUALS. THE BOARD EVALUATES THE PERFORMANCE OF THE PRESIDENT ANNUALLY AND MAKES RECOMMENDATIONS ON SALARY INCREASES BASED ON PERFORMANCE AND THE AVERAGE RATE OF INFLATION. COMPENSATION IS CONSIDERED AND APPROVED IN AN EXECUTIVE SESSION BY THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS. PERIODICALLY, HR REVIEWS COMPENSATION OF THE EXECUTIVE MANAGEMENT TEAM AND STAFF TO ENSURE THAT COMPENSATIONS ARE IN LINE WITH SIMILARLY QUALIFIED INDIVIDUALS IN COMPARABLE POSITIONS AT OTHER NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: UPON REQUEST, THE ORGANIZATION MAKES AVAILABLE THE DOCUMENTS REQUIRED TO BE MADE PUBLICLY AVAILABLE BY SECTION 6104.
PROGRAM CONSULTANTS: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 5,832,843. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 27,752. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 4. TOTAL EXPENSES 5,860,599.
